@@ -89,18 +89,21 @@ Current main already handles this request: non-native OpenAI Responses endpoints
 
 ## Close Comment
 
-Closing as implemented on `main` after a Codex review of current `main` (cec34821754a).
+Closing this as implemented after Codex review.
 
 Current main already handles this request: non-native OpenAI Responses endpoints strip `prompt_cache_key`/`prompt_cache_retention` automatically, and model compat now exposes `supportsPromptCacheKey` for explicit override. The changelog shows the automatic strip shipped in `2026.3.22` and the compat override landed later in unreleased notes.
 
-Evidence:
-- **Proxy-like Responses endpoints now default to stripping prompt cache fields:** `resolveProviderRequestCapabilities` sets `shouldStripResponsesPromptCache` for Responses APIs on explicit proxy-like endpoints, with `compat.supportsPromptCacheKey` as an override. (`src/agents/provider-attribution.ts:640`, `cec34821754a`)
-- **Payload policy deletes prompt cache fields when stripping is enabled:** `applyOpenAIResponsesPayloadPolicy` removes both `prompt_cache_key` and `prompt_cache_retention` when the resolved policy says to strip prompt cache. (`src/agents/openai-responses-payload-policy.ts:148`, `cec34821754a`)
-- **Regression tests cover the exact requested behavior:** Tests assert prompt cache fields are removed for non-OpenAI `openai-responses` endpoints and preserved for direct OpenAI/Azure OpenAI endpoints. (`src/agents/pi-embedded-runner-extraparams.test.ts:3354`, `cec34821754a`)
-- **Explicit model-level compat override exists:** `ModelCompatConfig` includes `supportsPromptCacheKey?: boolean`, which is the current config surface for forcing keep/strip behavior per model. (`src/config/types.models.ts:57`, `cec34821754a`)
-- **Changelog records both the shipped fix and later compat option:** `2026.3.22` notes the automatic stripping fix for non-OpenAI-compatible Responses endpoints, and unreleased notes add `models.providers.*.models.*.compat.supportsPromptCacheKey`. (`CHANGELOG.md:2156`, `cec34821754a`)
+What I checked:
+- Proxy-like Responses endpoints now default to stripping prompt cache fields: `resolveProviderRequestCapabilities` sets `shouldStripResponsesPromptCache` for Responses APIs on explicit proxy-like endpoints, with `compat.supportsPromptCacheKey` as an override. ([`src/agents/provider-attribution.ts:640`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/src/agents/provider-attribution.ts#L640), [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
+- Payload policy deletes prompt cache fields when stripping is enabled: `applyOpenAIResponsesPayloadPolicy` removes both `prompt_cache_key` and `prompt_cache_retention` when the resolved policy says to strip prompt cache. ([`src/agents/openai-responses-payload-policy.ts:148`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/src/agents/openai-responses-payload-policy.ts#L148), [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
+- Regression tests cover the exact requested behavior: Tests assert prompt cache fields are removed for non-OpenAI `openai-responses` endpoints and preserved for direct OpenAI/Azure OpenAI endpoints. ([`src/agents/pi-embedded-runner-extraparams.test.ts:3354`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/src/agents/pi-embedded-runner-extraparams.test.ts#L3354), [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
+- Explicit model-level compat override exists: `ModelCompatConfig` includes `supportsPromptCacheKey?: boolean`, which is the current config surface for forcing keep/strip behavior per model. ([`src/config/types.models.ts:57`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/src/config/types.models.ts#L57), [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
+- Changelog records both the shipped fix and later compat option: `2026.3.22` notes the automatic stripping fix for non-OpenAI-compatible Responses endpoints, and unreleased notes add `models.providers.*.models.*.compat.supportsPromptCacheKey`. ([`CHANGELOG.md:2156`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/CHANGELOG.md#L2156), [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
+
+So I’m closing this as already implemented rather than keeping a duplicate issue open.
+
+Review notes: reviewed against [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2); fixed evidence: release [`2026.3.22`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/2026.3.22).
 
-Codex evidence: Reviewed against main [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2). Fix evidence: release [2026.3.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/2026.3.22).
 
 ## GitHub Snapshot

Current main already renders safe inline markdown images in Control UI assistant replies, with tests and release notes showing it shipped in version 2026.2.13 and later security hardening preserved inline `data:image/...` rendering. -Evidence: -- **Assistant reply markdown goes through the sanitized markdown renderer:** Control UI chat bubbles render assistant/tool markdown via `toSanitizedMarkdownHtml(markdown)`, so markdown image support applies to agent replies. (`ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.ts:1477`, `cec34821754a`) -- **Markdown renderer emits inline image tags for safe markdown images:** `md.renderer.rules.image` returns `` for `data:image/...;base64,...` sources and flattens other URLs to alt text. (`ui/src/ui/markdown.ts:409`, `cec34821754a`) -- **Regression test covers inline markdown image rendering:** The markdown test suite asserts that `![Chart](data:image/png;base64,...)` produces an `` with the `markdown-inline-image` class. (`ui/src/ui/markdown.test.ts:285`, `cec34821754a`) -- **Release notes show the feature shipped:** The changelog for version `2026.2.13` says: `Web UI: add img to DOMPurify allowed tags and src/alt to allowed attributes so markdown images render in webchat instead of being stripped.`. (`CHANGELOG.md:4999`, `cec34821754a`) -- **Later hardening kept inline markdown images renderable:** A later changelog entry states remote markdown images were flattened to text while inline `data:image/...` markdown remained renderable across Control UI/webchat. (`CHANGELOG.md:3137`, `cec34821754a`) -- **Latest release still contains the implementation:** Comparing `v2026.4.22..HEAD` for the renderer/tests/styles showed only `docs/web/control-ui.md` changed; the markdown image implementation itself was already present in the latest release. +What I checked: +- Assistant reply markdown goes through the sanitized markdown renderer: Control UI chat bubbles render assistant/tool markdown via `toSanitizedMarkdownHtml(markdown)`, so markdown image support applies to agent replies. ([`ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.ts:1477`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.ts#L1477), [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a)) +- Markdown renderer emits inline image tags for safe markdown images: `md.renderer.rules.image` returns `` for `data:image/...;base64,...` sources and flattens other URLs to alt text. ([`ui/src/ui/markdown.ts:409`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/ui/src/ui/markdown.ts#L409), [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a)) +- Regression test covers inline markdown image rendering: The markdown test suite asserts that `![Chart](data:image/png;base64,...)` produces an `` with the `markdown-inline-image` class. ([`ui/src/ui/markdown.test.ts:285`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/ui/src/ui/markdown.test.ts#L285), [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a)) +- Release notes show the feature shipped: The changelog for version `2026.2.13` says: `Web UI: add img to DOMPurify allowed tags and src/alt to allowed attributes so markdown images render in webchat instead of being stripped.`. ([`CHANGELOG.md:4999`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/CHANGELOG.md#L4999), [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a)) +- Later hardening kept inline markdown images renderable: A later changelog entry states remote markdown images were flattened to text while inline `data:image/...` markdown remained renderable across Control UI/webchat. ([`CHANGELOG.md:3137`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/CHANGELOG.md#L3137), [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a)) +- Latest release still contains the implementation: Comparing `v2026.4.22..HEAD` for the renderer/tests/styles showed only `docs/web/control-ui.md` changed; the markdown image implementation itself was already present in the latest release. + +So I’m closing this as already implemented rather than keeping a duplicate issue open. + +Review notes: reviewed against [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2); fixed evidence: release [`2026.2.13`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/2026.2.13). -Codex evidence: Reviewed against main [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2). Fix evidence: release [2026.2.13](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/2026.2.13). ## GitHub Snapshot

@@ -93,19 +93,22 @@ Close as `clawhub`: the request is coherent and useful, but current `main` posit
 
 ## Close Comment
 
-Closing as better suited for ClawHub/community plugin work after a Codex review of current `main` (cec34821754a).
+Closing this as better suited for ClawHub/community plugin work after Codex review.
 
 Close as `clawhub`: the request is coherent and useful, but current `main` positions this kind of workspace-file integrity monitoring as extension/plugin territory rather than OpenClaw core.
 
-Evidence:
-- **Vision scopes optional capability to plugins:** The project vision says core should stay lean and that optional capability should usually ship as plugins, with a high bar for adding optional plugins to core. (`VISION.md:52`, `cec34821754a`)
-- **Hooks already cover bootstrap and startup lifecycle:** Internal hooks already expose `agent:bootstrap` and `gateway:startup`, which are the exact lifecycle points the issue asks for when hashing persona/bootstrap files at startup and during agent bootstrap. (`docs/automation/hooks.md:34`, `cec34821754a`)
-- **Hook events can emit user-visible alerts:** Hook handlers can push strings onto `event.messages`, and the internal hook event type defines `messages` specifically as messages sent back to the user. That matches the issue's proposed 'my brain was just replaced' alert behavior. (`src/hooks/internal-hook-types.ts:3`, `cec34821754a`)
-- **Bootstrap hooks receive the workspace bootstrap file set:** `applyBootstrapHookOverrides` passes `workspaceDir` and mutable `bootstrapFiles` through the `agent:bootstrap` event, so a hook/plugin can inspect or hash `AGENTS.md`/`SOUL.md`/`USER.md`-class files without core changes. (`src/agents/bootstrap-hooks.ts:7`, `cec34821754a`)
-- **Periodic checks already have a built-in scheduler:** Cron is a built-in scheduler that runs inside the Gateway and can deliver output to chat channels or webhooks, so recurring integrity verification does not need a new core scheduler surface. (`docs/automation/cron-jobs.md:10`, `cec34821754a`)
-- **The monitored files are operator-owned workspace content:** The agent workspace docs define `AGENTS.md`, `SOUL.md`, `USER.md`, and `IDENTITY.md` as private workspace files, and recommend backing that workspace with a private git repo. That makes this more of an operator-side integrity workflow than a core runtime contract. (`docs/concepts/agent-workspace.md:9`, `cec34821754a`)
+What I checked:
+- Vision scopes optional capability to plugins: The project vision says core should stay lean and that optional capability should usually ship as plugins, with a high bar for adding optional plugins to core. ([`VISION.md:52`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/VISION.md#L52), [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
+- Hooks already cover bootstrap and startup lifecycle: Internal hooks already expose `agent:bootstrap` and `gateway:startup`, which are the exact lifecycle points the issue asks for when hashing persona/bootstrap files at startup and during agent bootstrap. ([`docs/automation/hooks.md:34`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/docs/automation/hooks.md#L34), [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
+- Hook events can emit user-visible alerts: Hook handlers can push strings onto `event.messages`, and the internal hook event type defines `messages` specifically as messages sent back to the user. That matches the issue's proposed 'my brain was just replaced' alert behavior. ([`src/hooks/internal-hook-types.ts:3`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/src/hooks/internal-hook-types.ts#L3), [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
+- Bootstrap hooks receive the workspace bootstrap file set: `applyBootstrapHookOverrides` passes `workspaceDir` and mutable `bootstrapFiles` through the `agent:bootstrap` event, so a hook/plugin can inspect or hash `AGENTS.md`/`SOUL.md`/`USER.md`-class files without core changes. ([`src/agents/bootstrap-hooks.ts:7`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/src/agents/bootstrap-hooks.ts#L7), [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
+- Periodic checks already have a built-in scheduler: Cron is a built-in scheduler that runs inside the Gateway and can deliver output to chat channels or webhooks, so recurring integrity verification does not need a new core scheduler surface. ([`docs/automation/cron-jobs.md:10`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/docs/automation/cron-jobs.md#L10), [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
+- The monitored files are operator-owned workspace content: The agent workspace docs define `AGENTS.md`, `SOUL.md`, `USER.md`, and `IDENTITY.md` as private workspace files, and recommend backing that workspace with a private git repo. That makes this more of an operator-side integrity workflow than a core runtime contract. ([`docs/concepts/agent-workspace.md:9`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/docs/concepts/agent-workspace.md#L9), [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
+
+So I’m closing this as a scope-fit item for the plugin/community path rather than keeping it open as an OpenClaw core request.
+
+Review notes: reviewed against [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2).
 
-Codex evidence: Reviewed against main [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2). Specific fixed release/commit was not determined by this review.

@@ -87,18 +87,21 @@ Current main already routes subagent/sessions_spawn result messages through the
 
 ## Close Comment
 
-Closing as implemented on `main` after a Codex review of current `main` (cec34821754a).
+Closing this as implemented after Codex review.
 
 Current main already routes subagent/sessions_spawn result messages through the tool-result path and renders them as collapsible Tool output, not as plain user chat bubbles.
 
-Evidence:
-- **Tool-tagged messages normalize away from user role:** `normalizeMessage()` promotes any message carrying `toolCallId`, tool content blocks, or a tool name to `toolResult`, so the UI no longer relies on the original raw role for these payloads. (`ui/src/ui/chat/message-normalizer.ts:239`, `cec34821754a`)
-- **Renderer shows tool output UI:** `renderGroupedMessage()` treats `toolResult`/`tool_result` and any message with `toolCallId` as a tool message, labels it `Tool output`, and renders it in the collapsible tool-message container rather than a normal user bubble. (`ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.ts:1334`, `cec34821754a`)
-- **Direct sessions_spawn UI coverage:** UI test coverage explicitly renders a `sessions_spawn` result containing `childSessionKey` and asserts the transcript shows `Tool output` plus the structured result content, including the collapsible behavior. (`ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts:459`, `cec34821754a`)
-- **Subagent completion flow prefers tool output and ignores user text:** Subagent announce output extraction reads `toolResult`/`tool` messages from chat history, selects that output when assistant text is empty, and the e2e coverage verifies it falls back to tool output while explicitly not announcing user text. (`src/agents/subagent-announce-output.ts:152`, `cec34821754a`)
-- **Worktree stayed clean:** Read-only inspection left the checkout unchanged. (`cec34821754a`)
+What I checked:
+- Tool-tagged messages normalize away from user role: `normalizeMessage()` promotes any message carrying `toolCallId`, tool content blocks, or a tool name to `toolResult`, so the UI no longer relies on the original raw role for these payloads. ([`ui/src/ui/chat/message-normalizer.ts:239`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/ui/src/ui/chat/message-normalizer.ts#L239), [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
+- Renderer shows tool output UI: `renderGroupedMessage()` treats `toolResult`/`tool_result` and any message with `toolCallId` as a tool message, labels it `Tool output`, and renders it in the collapsible tool-message container rather than a normal user bubble. ([`ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.ts:1334`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.ts#L1334), [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
+- Direct sessions_spawn UI coverage: UI test coverage explicitly renders a `sessions_spawn` result containing `childSessionKey` and asserts the transcript shows `Tool output` plus the structured result content, including the collapsible behavior. ([`ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts:459`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts#L459), [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
+- Subagent completion flow prefers tool output and ignores user text: Subagent announce output extraction reads `toolResult`/`tool` messages from chat history, selects that output when assistant text is empty, and the e2e coverage verifies it falls back to tool output while explicitly not announcing user text. ([`src/agents/subagent-announce-output.ts:152`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/src/agents/subagent-announce-output.ts#L152), [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
+- Worktree stayed clean: Read-only inspection left the checkout unchanged. ([`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
+
+So I’m closing this as already implemented rather than keeping a duplicate issue open.
+
+Review notes: reviewed against [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2); fixed evidence: commit [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2).
 
-Codex evidence: Reviewed against main [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2). Fix evidence: commit [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2).

@@ -91,19 +91,21 @@ Current `main` already implements the requested hardening bundle, though not alw
 
 ## Close Comment
 
-Closing as implemented on `main` after Codex review.
+Closing this as implemented after Codex review.
 
-Current `main` already implements the requested hardening bundle. The exact workflow/file split differs from the proposal, but the substance is present: local Control UI assets, baseline gateway security headers, stricter Control UI headers, workflow linting, CodeQL, and dependency auditing.
+Current `main` already implements the requested hardening bundle, though not always with the exact workflow/file split proposed in the issue. The Control UI logo is local, gateway responses now get baseline security headers with stricter Control UI headers on top, and CI already enforces actionlint/zizmor plus CodeQL and dependency auditing.
+
+What I checked:
+- Local Control UI asset: `agentLogoUrl` now resolves to the bundled `favicon.svg`, and the asset exists in-repo. ([`ui/src/ui/views/agents-utils.ts:243`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/ui/src/ui/views/agents-utils.ts#L243), [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
+- Baseline gateway security headers: `server-http` applies `setDefaultSecurityHeaders` to HTTP requests, and that helper sets `X-Content-Type-Options`, `Referrer-Policy`, and `Permissions-Policy`. ([`src/gateway/server-http.ts:900`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/src/gateway/server-http.ts#L900), [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
+- Control UI hardening headers: Control UI responses additionally set `X-Frame-Options: DENY` and a CSP, with tests asserting those headers. ([`src/gateway/control-ui.ts:142`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/src/gateway/control-ui.ts#L142), [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
+- CI enforces actionlint and zizmor: `workflow-sanity.yml` runs actionlint, and `ci.yml` audits changed workflows with zizmor. ([`.github/workflows/workflow-sanity.yml:52`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/.github/workflows/workflow-sanity.yml#L52), [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
+- Dependency/security scanning is present: A dedicated CodeQL workflow exists, and main CI also runs production dependency auditing. ([`.github/workflows/codeql.yml:1`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/.github/workflows/codeql.yml#L1), [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))

 ## Close Comment
 
-Closing as implemented on `main` after a Codex review of current `main` (cec34821754a).
+Closing this as implemented after Codex review.
 
 Current main already implements progressive, context-aware skill disclosure with bounded prompt cost: the model scans a lightweight `available_skills` catalog and reads only the matching `SKILL.md` when needed, with compact/truncated catalog fallback and mid-session snapshot refreshes for watcher/allowlist changes. That addresses the token-cost request even though the shipped design differs from the issue's trigger-frontmatter sketch.
 
-Evidence:
-- **System prompt uses lazy skill loading:** The agent is instructed to scan `available_skills`, then read exactly one matching `SKILL.md` only after selection; if none apply it reads none. (`src/agents/system-prompt.ts:162`, `cec34821754a`)
-- **Prompt contains only skill catalog metadata, not full skill bodies:** `formatSkillsForPrompt` injects only each skill's name, description, and location into the prompt catalog. (`src/agents/skills/skill-contract.ts:44`, `cec34821754a`)
-- **Prompt budget compacts and truncates the catalog:** When the full catalog is too large, OpenClaw switches to compact formatting or truncates the list with an explicit warning, directly addressing token pressure. (`src/agents/skills/workspace.ts:791`, `cec34821754a`)
-- **Docs quantify low deterministic token overhead:** The skills docs describe the session snapshot model and document that the prompt cost is a compact XML catalog with 195 chars base overhead plus 97 chars per skill plus field lengths, not full skill text. (`docs/tools/skills.md:331`, `cec34821754a`)
-- **Snapshot refreshes when eligibility changes:** Current main rebuilds the session skill snapshot when the snapshot version changes or the effective agent skill filter changes, and the docs also describe watcher-driven mid-session hot reload. (`src/agents/agent-command.ts:575`, `cec34821754a`)

 Current `main` already implements agent-driven model switching well enough: agents have a first-class `session_status(model=...)` tool, the system prompt explicitly advertises that override and tells agents to use tools instead of equivalent slash/CLI commands, and the runtime/test coverage handles live-switch retries for active runs.

-- **Agent prompt advertises model override on `session_status`:** The injected tool summary tells the agent that `session_status` can answer model-state questions and set an optional per-session model override. (`src/agents/system-prompt.ts:507`, `1daa552d5f42`)

-- **Runtime supports deferred live model switches:** During an active attempt, a pending live switch is detected and retried via `LiveSessionModelSwitchError` when restart is safe. (`src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.ts:879`, `1daa552d5f42`)
-- **Tests cover override persistence and live switch behavior:** Tests verify `session_status` can set a model override on the current session and that live switches are queued for active runs. (`src/agents/openclaw-tools.session-status.test.ts:778`, `1daa552d5f42`)
-- **Docs describe the agent-facing override surface:** The session tools docs now describe `session_status` as a `/status`-style tool that can set a per-session model override and clear it with `model=default`. (`docs/concepts/session-tool.md:81`, `1daa552d5f42`)

@@ -100,19 +100,22 @@ Current main already provides the requested safety model: host `exec` is guarded
 
 ## Close Comment
 
-Closing as implemented on `main` after a Codex review of current `main` (cec34821754a).
+Closing this as implemented after Codex review.
 
 Current main already provides the requested safety model: host `exec` is guarded by built-in exec approvals/allowlists/ask-fallback, and plugins can intercept any tool call with `before_tool_call` to either block it or require human approval. The exact YAML sketch from the issue was not added, but the underlying capability is present and shipped.
 
-Evidence:
-- **Built-in exec approvals guard host command execution:** The docs describe exec approvals as a host guardrail where commands run only when policy, allowlist, and optional user approval all agree; the effective policy is the stricter of `tools.exec.*` and `~/.openclaw/exec-approvals.json`, with deny fallback when no UI is available. (`docs/tools/exec-approvals.md:10`, `cec34821754a`)
-- **Runtime enforces stricter host exec policy:** `resolveExecHostApprovalContext` merges requested exec policy with host approvals and throws when the effective host security resolves to `deny`, so host config can tighten and block execution. (`src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-shared.ts:198`, `cec34821754a`)
-- **Plugin hook contract supports blocking or approval-gating tool calls:** `PluginHookBeforeToolCallResult` includes both `block`/`blockReason` and `requireApproval` fields with approval metadata, which is the generic interception seam for tool safety policies. (`src/plugins/hook-types.ts:324`, `cec34821754a`)
-- **Agent loop pauses tool execution for human approval:** When a `before_tool_call` hook returns `requireApproval`, the agent requests `plugin.approval.request`, waits on `plugin.approval.waitDecision`, allows on `allow-once`/`allow-always`, blocks on `deny`, and blocks or allows on timeout according to the configured timeout behavior. (`src/agents/pi-tools.before-tool-call.ts:219`, `cec34821754a`)
-- **Gateway exposes plugin approval flow for human-in-the-loop resolution:** The gateway implements `plugin.approval.request/list/waitDecision/resolve`, creates approval records, and forwards approval requests for delivery to approval clients. (`src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-approval.ts:26`, `cec34821754a`)
-- **Docs describe approval UX across channels:** Plugin docs state that `before_tool_call: { requireApproval: true }` pauses execution and prompts through the exec approval overlay, Telegram buttons, Discord interactions, or `/approve` on any channel. (`docs/plugins/building-plugins.md:184`, `cec34821754a`)

 ## Close Comment
 
-Closing as better suited for ClawHub/community plugin work after a Codex review of current `main` (cec34821754a).
+Closing this as better suited for ClawHub/community plugin work after Codex review.
 
 The request is a real idea, but current `main` treats this as community-plugin territory rather than an OpenClaw core cleanup item: new channel integrations are documented as plugins, community/ClawHub is the canonical distribution path, and the docs already point to an external Vapi bridge.
 
-Evidence:
-- **Vision keeps optional integrations out of core:** Current main says optional capability should usually ship as plugins, preferably hosted and maintained outside this repo, with a high bar for adding optional plugins to core. (`VISION.md:54`, `cec34821754a`)
-- **Vision scope guardrails match this request:** The roadmap guardrails explicitly list commercial service integrations and wrapper channels as things OpenClaw will not merge for now unless there is unusually strong rationale. (`VISION.md:101`, `cec34821754a`)
-- **New channels are expected to be plugins:** The channel-plugin guide says a new messaging platform should be built as a ChannelPlugin, with core keeping the shared message tool while the plugin owns config, security, pairing, outbound delivery, and threading. (`docs/plugins/sdk-channel-plugins.md:11`, `cec34821754a`)
-- **Community/ClawHub is the canonical path:** Current docs say community plugins can extend OpenClaw with channels and that ClawHub is the canonical discovery surface for community plugins. (`docs/plugins/community.md:9`, `cec34821754a`)
-- **Repo already points to an external Vapi bridge:** The showcase on current main includes 'Clawdia phone bridge', described as a 'Vapi voice assistant to OpenClaw HTTP bridge' for near real-time phone calls. (`docs/start/showcase.md:282`, `cec34821754a`)
-- **Telephony already lives in plugin land:** Voice calling is documented as an installable plugin rather than a core channel, reinforcing that call transports belong in plugins. (`docs/plugins/voice-call.md:9`, `cec34821754a`)
+What I checked:
+- Vision keeps optional integrations out of core: Current main says optional capability should usually ship as plugins, preferably hosted and maintained outside this repo, with a high bar for adding optional plugins to core. ([`VISION.md:54`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/VISION.md#L54), [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
+- Vision scope guardrails match this request: The roadmap guardrails explicitly list commercial service integrations and wrapper channels as things OpenClaw will not merge for now unless there is unusually strong rationale. ([`VISION.md:101`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/VISION.md#L101), [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
+- New channels are expected to be plugins: The channel-plugin guide says a new messaging platform should be built as a ChannelPlugin, with core keeping the shared message tool while the plugin owns config, security, pairing, outbound delivery, and threading. ([`docs/plugins/sdk-channel-plugins.md:11`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/docs/plugins/sdk-channel-plugins.md#L11), [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
+- Community/ClawHub is the canonical path: Current docs say community plugins can extend OpenClaw with channels and that ClawHub is the canonical discovery surface for community plugins. ([`docs/plugins/community.md:9`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/docs/plugins/community.md#L9), [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
+- Repo already points to an external Vapi bridge: The showcase on current main includes 'Clawdia phone bridge', described as a 'Vapi voice assistant to OpenClaw HTTP bridge' for near real-time phone calls. ([`docs/start/showcase.md:282`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/docs/start/showcase.md#L282), [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
+- Telephony already lives in plugin land: Voice calling is documented as an installable plugin rather than a core channel, reinforcing that call transports belong in plugins. ([`docs/plugins/voice-call.md:9`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754a/docs/plugins/voice-call.md#L9), [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754a))
+
+So I’m closing this as a scope-fit item for the plugin/community path rather than keeping it open as an OpenClaw core request.
+
+Review notes: reviewed against [`cec34821754a`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2).
